title: Truist Cuts SoFi Price Target to $17: Loan Platform Slowdown Pressures the Bull Case
text: Truist cut its SoFi price target to $17 following Q1 2026 earnings, citing slowdown pressures in the loan platform business. The technology platform segment declined 27% year-over-year due to a large customer departure, raising concerns about the platform's sustainability. This weakness in the technology segment challenged the bull case for SoFi as investors had been excited about the company's ability to diversify beyond direct lending. Truist's downgrade joined other analyst cuts including Goldman Sachs (target $17 from $20) and Morgan Stanley (target $16 from higher levels). However, the underlying personal lending business remained strong with record originations of $8.3 billion in Q1. The loan platform business itself added $3.6 billion in new partner commitments in Q1, suggesting demand from third-party lenders remains solid. Despite the analyst downgrades, the company's core lending metrics and overall profitability trajectory remained intact. The market's reaction highlighted investor concern about near-term execution risks and the company's reliance on technology platform revenue contribution.
